# copilot-instructions-template

> A Lean/Kaizen-inspired GitHub Copilot instructions template that installs a structured, self-updating AI agent workflow into any VS Code project. Covers operating modes, PDCA cycle, waste classification, heartbeat health checks, model-pinned agents, hooks, MCP servers, tools, and skills.

## Key files

- [.github/copilot-instructions.md](.github/copilot-instructions.md): Main always-loaded system prompt (§1–§13). Start here.
- [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md): Machine-readable entry point for AI agents — trigger phrases, bootstrap sequence, update protocol.
- [UPDATE.md](UPDATE.md): Protocol for fetching and applying upstream template updates (version-walk, three-way merge, companion files).
- [MIGRATION.md](MIGRATION.md): Per-version migration registry — drives the version-walk update algorithm.
- [SETUP.md](SETUP.md): Human-readable first-time setup guide.
- [docs/AGENTS-GUIDE.md](docs/AGENTS-GUIDE.md): Human reference for agents, model assignments, fallback chains, handoffs, and sub-directory instruction scoping.
- [docs/HOOKS-GUIDE.md](docs/HOOKS-GUIDE.md): Hook configuration format, available events, security considerations.

## Agents (`.github/agents/`)

| File | Model | Purpose |
|------|-------|---------|
| `setup.agent.md` | Claude Sonnet 4.6 | First-time setup and onboarding |
| `coding.agent.md` | GPT-5.3-Codex | Implementation, refactoring, multi-step tasks |
| `review.agent.md` | Claude Opus 4.6 | Lean/Kaizen code review |
| `fast.agent.md` | Claude Haiku 4.5 | Quick questions and lightweight edits |
| `update.agent.md` | Claude Sonnet 4.6 | Upstream instruction update workflow |
| `doctor.agent.md` | Claude Sonnet 4.6 | Read-only health check on all config files |

## Skills (`.github/skills/` after install, `template/skills/` in this repo)

| Skill | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `conventional-commit` | Author a Conventional Commits-compliant commit message |
| `lean-pr-review` | Review a pull request using Lean waste categories |
| `fix-ci-failure` | Diagnose and fix a failing CI run |
| `skill-creator` | Author a new SKILL.md skill file |
| `mcp-builder` | Build or configure a new MCP server |
| `webapp-testing` | Write and run web application tests |
| `issue-triage` | Triage a GitHub issue with severity, waste category, and recommended action |

## MCP servers (`.vscode/mcp.json` after install)

| Server | Purpose |
|--------|---------|
| `@modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem` | File operations beyond the workspace |
| `@modelcontextprotocol/server-memory` | Ephemeral session-scoped knowledge graph |
| `mcp-server-git` | Git history, diffs, and branch operations |
| `@modelcontextprotocol/server-github` | GitHub API — issues, PRs, repos, actions |
| `mcp-server-fetch` | HTTP fetch for web content and APIs |

## Workspace identity files (`.copilot/workspace/` after install)

- `HEARTBEAT.md` — health check checklist and pulse history
- `MEMORY.md` — architectural decisions, error patterns, team conventions
- `SOUL.md` — agent reasoning alignment and heuristics
- `USER.md` — user preferences observed over time
- `IDENTITY.md` — agent self-description
- `TOOLS.md` — effective tool usage patterns
- `BOOTSTRAP.md` — permanent setup origin record

## Project tracking files (project root after install)

- `JOURNAL.md` — chronological log of significant decisions and events
- `BIBLIOGRAPHY.md` — complete catalogue of all project files
- `METRICS.md` — LOC baselines, test coverage, deployment frequency

## Optional: full docs

- [docs/](docs/): Detailed guides for hooks, agents, MCP, skills, and setup.
- [.github/hooks/](template/hooks/): Lifecycle hook scripts (session-start, guard-destructive, post-edit-lint, enforce-retrospective, save-context).
